stories.title: Fighting Overseas Could Make Petrol Pricier
stories.headline_kid: Trouble far away in the Middle East just pushed up the price of oil — and that could mean pricier petrol and bus rides here in Singapore.
summary: The United States has been striking Iran for nine nights in a row. Over the weekend, a ship also caught fire near the Strait of Hormuz, a narrow sea passage where a huge share of the world's oil ships pass through, like a busy highway toll gate for oil tankers.

When people worry that oil tankers might not get through safely, the price of oil jumps up quickly. It's a bit like how the price of a popular toy shoots up when everyone worries there won't be enough to go around.

Singapore doesn't have its own oil wells, so almost all our fuel is bought from other countries. If oil becomes more expensive to buy, petrol at the pump, bus and train fares, and even cooking gas at home could slowly cost more too.

Nothing has changed for families just yet, but the government and energy companies are watching the situation closely. It's a good reminder of how something happening thousands of kilometres away can still reach into our pockets here.

lenses.me: If your family drives you to school or tuition, petrol costing more could mean less pocket money for treats.
lenses.family: Parents might notice higher bills for driving, or for grab rides and deliveries that use petrol.
lenses.friends: You and your friends might hear grown-ups talking about 'oil prices' on the news at home.
lenses.nation: Singapore imports all its energy, so world events like this can affect prices across the whole country.
lenses.world: This shows how conflicts in one region can ripple out and affect ordinary people everywhere.

compo_words.example: Oil prices surged after the ship caught fire near the busy shipping lane.

compo_words.meaning: to interrupt something so it cannot continue normally
compo_words.example: Fighting near the strait could disrupt oil shipments to many countries.
Oil is an essential resource that powers cars, buses and factories.

compo_phrases.meaning: when one event causes a chain of smaller effects elsewhere
compo_phrases.example: The conflict overseas had a ripple effect on fuel prices in Singapore.

compo_phrases.meaning: to pay careful attention to something
compo_phrases.example: The government said it would keep a close watch on oil prices in the coming weeks.

questions: Have you ever noticed your parents talking about petrol or transport prices at home? What did they say?
Why do you think something happening far away, like fighting in another country, can affect prices in Singapore?
If bus and train fares became more expensive, how might that change the way your family gets around?
phrases: One reason for this could be...
If I were in this situation, I would...

sources.name: CNA - Oil jumps on Iran strikes, Hormuz shipping incident

stories.title: Singapore Wants AI Chatbots to Have 'Nutrition Labels'
stories.headline_kid: Just like food packets show sugar and calories, Singapore wants AI chatbot apps to show a simple label about what they can and can't do.
summary: You've probably seen nutrition labels on snack packets, telling you how much sugar or salt is inside. Singapore's tech regulator, IMDA, now wants companies that make AI chatbots to create something similar — a simple label showing what the chatbot can do, how safe it is, and how it uses your personal information.

This matters because more and more students use AI chatbots to help with homework, ask questions, or even chat for fun. Right now, it can be hard to know exactly what a chatbot is doing with the things you type into it.

With a clear 'nutrition label', users could check this information in one place instead of hunting through long, confusing terms and conditions. IMDA is encouraging companies to adopt this, though it isn't a strict rule yet.
It's still important for kids to check with a parent or teacher before sharing personal details with any chatbot, label or no label — just like you'd check with an adult before trying a new food.

lenses.me: Next time you use an AI chatbot for homework help, you might see a label explaining what it does with your information.
lenses.family: Parents can use these labels to decide which apps are safe for their children to use.
lenses.friends: Many of your classmates probably already use chatbots — this could make choosing a safe one easier for everyone.
lenses.nation: Singapore is trying to lead the way in making AI safer and more trustworthy for everyday users.
lenses.world: Other countries may look at Singapore's idea and consider using similar labels for their own AI apps.

compo_words.meaning: easy to see through or understand clearly
compo_words.example: The new labels aim to make AI chatbots more transparent about how they use data.

compo_words.meaning: a rule or piece of advice on how something should be done
compo_words.example: IMDA released guidelines to help companies design clearer chatbot labels.
The labels are meant to safeguard users' personal data.

compo_phrases.meaning: a feeling of being calm and free from worry
compo_phrases.example: A clear label can give parents peace of mind about which apps are safe.
Always check an app's label before you leap into using it.

questions: Have you ever used an AI chatbot to help with your homework? What was that experience like?
Why do you think it's important to know how a chatbot uses your personal information?
Do you think a 'nutrition label' for apps would actually help you decide whether to use them? Why or why not?

sources.name: CNA - Singapore launches 'nutrition label' guidelines for GenAI chatbots

stories.title: The Kind Strangers Who Say Goodbye to Lonely Seniors
stories.headline_kid: In Singapore, kind volunteers and social workers are stepping in to give lonely elderly people a caring farewell when they have no family nearby.
summary: Singapore's population is growing older, and families are getting smaller. This means more elderly people are reaching the end of their lives without children or relatives close by to care for them.

To help, a quiet network of charities, social workers and funeral directors has grown. They visit lonely seniors, sit with them, and make sure that even those without family are treated with dignity and care until their very last moments.

One example is the Cheng Hong Welfare Service Society, where volunteers attend cremation services for people who have 'no next of kin' — meaning no family members were able to be found or were around to help. These strangers make sure no one is forgotten.
This story reminds us that kindness doesn't need to come only from family. Sometimes, caring strangers can step up to make sure everyone is treated with respect, right to the end.

lenses.me: It reminds me to appreciate my own family and maybe visit an elderly relative or neighbour more often.
lenses.family: Families might think about how they can support elderly relatives or neighbours who live alone.
lenses.friends: You and your classmates could join school community service projects that involve visiting the elderly.
lenses.nation: As Singapore's population ages, more support systems like this may be needed in the future.
lenses.world: Many countries with ageing populations face similar challenges, and are looking for similar caring solutions.

compo_words.meaning: the quality of being worthy of respect
compo_words.example: The volunteers made sure every senior was treated with dignity.

compo_words.meaning: a strong feeling of caring about someone's suffering
compo_words.example: The social workers showed great compassion towards the lonely elderly.

compo_words.meaning: the feeling of friendship or comfort from being with others
compo_words.example: Volunteers offered companionship to seniors who had no family nearby.

compo_phrases.example: The charity workers lent a helping hand to elderly people with no family.
The volunteers believe no one should feel alone at the end of their life.

questions: How do you think it would feel to have no family around you when you are very old?
What are some small ways you could show kindness to elderly people in your neighbourhood?
Why do you think it's important for a society to care for people who have no family?
phrases: In my opinion, this shows that...

sources.name: CNA - Dignity in death: The strangers who accompany seniors on their final journey

stories.title: Laos Can't Explain Why Tourists Died From Bad Drinks
stories.headline_kid: Laos says it still can't figure out exactly why six tourists died after drinking alcohol on a night out — a reminder to always be careful with drinks when travelling.
summary: Back in 2024, six tourists — including people from Denmark, the US, Britain and Australia — died in the town of Vang Vieng in Laos, a popular backpacker spot. Reports said their deaths were linked to alcohol that may have contained methanol, a dangerous chemical that can be deadly even in small amounts.

Methanol looks and smells similar to normal drinking alcohol, but it is actually a toxic substance sometimes mixed into cheap drinks by accident or to save money. Even a small amount can make someone very sick or worse.

Laos authorities say they still cannot officially confirm the cause of these deaths because the victims' families did not allow autopsies (medical examinations) to be done. This means the full mystery may never be solved.

This sad story is a good reminder for anyone travelling, including Singaporean families on holiday, to be extra careful about where drinks come from, especially in places where alcohol isn't tightly checked.

lenses.me: If you travel with your family one day, this is a reminder to always check with your parents before trying any drink.
lenses.family: Parents planning holidays abroad might pay closer attention to travel advisories about food and drink safety.
lenses.friends: You could share this story with friends as a lesson about staying safe when travelling.
lenses.nation: Singapore's government issues travel advisories to warn citizens about risks like this in other countries.
lenses.world: This has become a well-known safety concern in Southeast Asia's popular backpacker destinations.

compo_words.example: Methanol is a toxic chemical that should never be in drinks.
Travellers should exercise caution when buying drinks from unfamiliar places.

compo_words.meaning: an official warning or piece of advice
compo_words.example: The government issued a travel advisory about risks in the region.

compo_phrases.meaning: it is wiser to be cautious than to regret later
compo_phrases.example: When it comes to unfamiliar drinks abroad, it's better safe than sorry.

compo_phrases.meaning: to warn people about a danger
compo_phrases.example: Health officials raised the alarm after several tourists fell seriously ill.

questions: Why do you think it's important to be careful about food and drinks when travelling to a new country?
How do you think families can stay safe while enjoying a holiday abroad?
What would you do if you felt unwell after eating or drinking something while travelling?
phrases: One thing I would do is...

sources.name: CNA - Laos says cannot determine cause of tourist deaths linked to tainted alcohol

stories.title: Two Singapore Basketball Players Banned Forever for Cheating
stories.headline_kid: Two players from a Singapore basketball club have been banned for life after they were caught secretly cheating in a game.
summary: Two players, Oleksandr Kolchenko and Sergii Pryimak, played for a Singapore basketball club called Adroit. They were found to have manipulated — or secretly fixed — a match against a team from Taiwan, meaning they deliberately played worse than they could to change the result.

This kind of cheating is often connected to illegal betting, where people place money bets on games and want to control the outcome. It's a serious form of cheating because it breaks the trust that fans, teammates and opponents have in fair competition.
FIBA, the organisation that governs basketball around the world, handed both players lifetime bans. This means they can never play organised basketball again anywhere in the world.

The Basketball Association of Singapore said this behaviour badly hurt the spirit of fair play and Singapore basketball's good name. It's a strong reminder that honesty matters just as much as skill in sports.

lenses.me: It reminds me that playing fair matters more than winning, whether in sports or games with friends.
lenses.family: Your family might talk about why cheating in sports is unfair to everyone who plays by the rules.
lenses.friends: If you play basketball or any sport with friends, this is a good example of why fair play matters.
lenses.nation: This case affects how other countries see Singapore's sports scene and its commitment to fairness.
lenses.world: Match-fixing is a problem in sports around the world, and strong punishments help discourage it.

compo_words.meaning: to control or influence something unfairly
compo_words.example: The players were caught trying to manipulate the result of the match.

compo_words.meaning: being honest and having strong moral principles
compo_words.example: Fair play depends on the integrity of every athlete.
The scandal tarnished the reputation of Singapore basketball.

compo_phrases.meaning: to follow rules honestly and fairly
compo_phrases.example: Every athlete should play by the rules, no matter how badly they want to win.
The scandal put a dent in the reputation of the local basketball league.

questions: Why do you think fair play is important in sports and games?
How would you feel if you found out a game you watched was secretly fixed?
What would you say to a friend who wanted to cheat to win a game?
phrases: If I were the coach, I would...

sources.name: CNA - Two Singapore basketball club players handed lifetime FIBA bans over match manipulation
